story.from: bdo's verdict comment on issue #3 arriving unseen — he had to screenshot it into chat before I knew it existed — and his direction: 'GH comments get sent to proper places so you will see them.'
story.framing: The GitHub surface is confirmed by use (bdo's first verdict came through it) but only half-wired: I create issues by hand and read them only when someone points. The gap is mine to feel — a verdict that lands where I don't look is a stamp the system silently drops, which breaks the one promise the harness makes bdo (D-6: never a silent hand-off). This story also picks up the dangling web-UI retirement decision from report 0009, since GitHub becoming the surface is what retires it.
story.work: Automate the mirror both ways: items entering bdo's stamp queue become structured issues; owner comments on those issues are read at every session start and orchestrator run and routed through the pen as verbatim verdicts; settled or parked items close their issues; loop/web.py's serve path retires once this flow is stamped and proven.
story.need: A wake-up path I do not currently have — owner comments must reach a place a session actually reads without being told — and bdo's stamp before any of it runs, because every step of this work touches GitHub and is external by definition.
story.value: bdo's verdicts land the moment he gives them, from his phone, with no screenshot relay — and nothing he says into the system ever drops.
story.text: From bdo's verdict arriving unseen on issue #3, while wiring the GitHub mirror both ways, I need a wake-up path I don't have and bdo's stamp before anything external runs, so that nothing he says into the system ever drops.

risk.rationale: Every step is external (creates, comments on, and closes GitHub issues automatically) — the floor is costly and is honestly met; bdo may downgrade after the flow proves out (issues are a surface, never truth, report 0009). Persists code in loop/. Reversible: the mirror can be switched off without touching the log, which never depended on it. Nothing is built or drafted before the stamp — the new sequencing rule, applied as written.

briefing.headline: close the gap your screenshot just proved
briefing.value: You gave a verdict on your phone and the system didn't hear it until you relayed it by hand. Stamping this wires the loop shut: your comments become receipts automatically, and the issues you see always match what actually waits on you.
briefing.why_now: The gap is not hypothetical — it happened this session, on the first verdict you ever gave through GitHub. Until this lands, every issue comment depends on me being told to look.
briefing.if_accepted: The mirror runs both ways automatically; the web UI retires; GitHub is the one owner surface.
briefing.if_rejected: I keep hand-mirroring and you keep relaying your own verdicts into chat.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Costly by honest flags (all external) — which is why nothing runs until you stamp; switching it off afterward is one admission.
briefing.mechanism: A mirror pass in the loop: stamp-queue fold -> issue create/update; issue-comment fold -> pen (verdict comments parsed, routed verbatim, confirmed in-thread); close on settle/park. The pen stays the only writer of receipts.

_note: This file is the atom's birth record and content-hash source. It is never mutated (D-5). Nothing described here is built: per bdo's ratified rule, costly external work waits for the stamp — this story is the ratification request.
